If you are truly wanted to develop in this field for long run, don't enter the field when you are still fresh grad. There are too many "green" candidates joined the industry in recent years and the pay is getting lower and lower for the entry levels. It takes so long for getting promoted for a certain grade.

Suggest to get in either front line in bank (Corp/institutional banking/AM/PB) / LEA (SFC/HKMA /ICAC (Operations)/ Police (CCB/JFIU) as a stepping stone to help yourself switch to the industry with better rank and salary.

Duties are routine and boring as well no matter you are working in either KYC/CDD (including onboarding & EDD)/AML(tran mon, tran screening)/Sanctions. Things get slightly interesting if you move to an advising role to work along with the front line. If you are the teamheads / manager of manager, you may not need to work on case however the MI/Escalation you face would be more tedious.

Salary really depends on bank types/ business segment (i.e. in HSBC RBWM/BB/Corp/GB/PB) / scope (local, regional, global) that you joining, and the "seafood price" situation is common. I have seen people working on the same team with same grade doing exact7ly the same thing could have a salary deviation of HKD50k (manager grade).

p.s. only apply for FCC

quote.msg:
If you are truly wanted to develop in this field for long run, don't enter the field when you are still fresh grad. There are too many "green" candidates joined the industry in recent years and the pay is getting lower and lower for the entry levels. It takes so long for getting promoted for a certain grade.

Suggest to get in either front line in bank (Corp/institutional banking/AM/PB) / LEA (SFC/HKMA /ICAC (Operations)/ Police (CCB/JFIU) as a stepping stone to help yourself switch to the industry with better rank and salary.

Duties are routine and boring as well no matter you are working in either KYC/CDD (including onboarding & EDD)/AML(tran mon, tran screening)/Sanctions. Things get slightly interesting if you move to an advising role to work along with the front line. If you are the teamheads / manager of manager, you may not need to work on case however the MI/Escalation you face would be more tedious.

Salary really depends on bank types/ business segment (i.e. in HSBC RBWM/BB/Corp/GB/PB) / scope (local, regional, global) that you joining, and the "seafood price" situation is common. I have seen people working on the same team with same grade doing exact7ly the same thing could have a salary deviation of HKD50k (manager grade).

p.s. only apply for FCC
